QF.id: XVI Clearing, Settlement and Risk Management_44

question.text: Quality certified goods must be deposited in the warehouse by the seller:

answer.text: Before the initiation of the delivery process — warehouse receipt is needed for pay-in
Only after the buyer confirms acceptance
At any time within 30 days of expiry
Quality certification is not required for delivery

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The seller must deposit quality-certified goods and receive a warehouse receipt BEFORE initiating the delivery process. The receipt is transferred to the CC during pay-in. **The Trap:** Buyer confirmation isn't needed first. Not 30 days post-expiry. Quality certification IS required. **Cross-Exam Context:** Delivery logistics are specific to commodity markets. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Start the delivery preparation process well before the tender period — quality certification and warehousing take time, and delays can lead to delivery default.
